title: Update on access to Piqray/clinical trial
body:
I'm Stage 4 MBC. Posted before about getting compassionate access to piqray. No go. But I can self fund it, which I fortunately do have the money for.  Tje money was supposed to be for fixing my house after the NSW floods, but hey, staying alive to do the renos is more important. 

I decided not to do the clinical trial because I am often too tired to drive to the city every 3 weeks. I went up for an appt last week and had to nap in my car. Plus there is no guarantee I would be in the Piqray treatment group. It's been hard to make such a big decision, with my oncologist and others having different ideas, but I have to do what feels right for me. 

Onco also said he would sign my life insurance forms. They only pay out if you have less than 12 months to live!  I'm not feeling that's me. I think I have two years at least. But hey, I get to spend my money while I'm still here! And I'm not sick. Just tired. 

Plus tonight I'm having a Zoom appointment with Care Oncology in the UK, who do off label and other treatments, so I'm excited for that. It's expensive and it's taken me a long time to get the paperwork to them. I kind of resent having to do all this medical stuff and procrastinate a lot. But they do amazing things. 

Anyway, that's me. Feeling hopeful.
